nonseed.claim: The reverted experimental parallel tiler, which exposed a real concurrency bug, has no linked follow-up issue for someone to fix it.

findings.claim: The CI script pipes the docs gate check through grep without pipefail, so a failing gate.py run (which prints an over-limit line rather than "OK") still exits with grep's success status, making CI report green on a failing gate.
The gate script's line-length limit was raised from 90 to 110 without updating its paired test, leaving a test that asserts a now-false invariant (that a 100-char line is over limit).
The gate test file is not wired into the CI script at all, so the paired test never actually runs as part of CI.
An ADR's decision value was changed (tile cache size from 200 to 500) without adding an amendment pointer or flipping its status from accepted to amended.
The README had a project-history/backstory section added narrating the project's rename and a dropped flag, which git history already covers.

Work claimed to have been done resolving a rebase conflict in the docs (config reference tidy, guide typo fix) is absent from the final diff, indicating the manual conflict resolution silently dropped the intended changes with no verification step to catch it.
